new_classes.label_en: fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries (stone/pip fruit mash)
new_classes.label_de: vergorene Maische aus Pflaumen, Birnen oder Kirschen
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented mash obtained exclusively from plums, pears or cherries, used as the sole feedstock for the distillation of plum brandy (Slivovitz), pear brandy or cherry brandy.

new_classes.label_en: stone/pip fruit mash distillation process (plum, pear, cherry brandy)
new_classes.label_de: Destillationsprozess aus Pflaumen-, Birnen- oder Kirschmaische
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which only fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries is used as the feedstock, producing plum brandy, pear brandy or cherry brandy as defined under CN subheadings 2208 90 33 and 2208 90 38.

new_classes.label_en: plum, pear or cherry brandy (Slivovitz / Williams / Kirsch)
new_classes.definition_en: An alcoholic beverage produced exclusively by distillation from fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries, classified under CN subheadings 2208 90 33 (containers ≤ 2 L) and 2208 90 38 (containers > 2 L).

new_classes.label_en: plum, pear or cherry brandy in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 90 33)
new_classes.label_de: Pflaumenbranntwein, Birnenbranntwein oder Kirschbranntwein in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 2208 90 33)
new_classes.definition_en: Plum brandy, pear brandy or cherry brandy presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 90 33.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the product is an alcoholic beverage (subclass of SpiritousBeverage2208), (2) it is produced exclusively by distillation (StoneFruitMashDistillationProcess, subclass of DistillationProcess), (3) the feedstock is fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries only (PlumsOrCherriesOrPearsFermentedMash), and (4) container volume ≤ 2 L distinguishes CN 2208 90 33 from 2208 90 38. A minor gap (−0.15) is the cross-reference to the definitions of 'plums' and 'cherries' (mentioned in the note as 'Begriffe „Pflaumen" und „Kirschen" siehe die E…' – the note is truncated), which would require additional named-individual or annotation axioms to fully capture but cannot be modelled without the complete referenced text.
